summary: Replace dispatch-time Call Compile on sender-provided PVF handler strings with validated lookup of precompiled SRVFNC/CLTFNC handlers.
implementationShape: Look up precompiled handler CODE in missionNamespace after validating against registered server/client command lists.
Reject unknown or non-CODE handlers with compact WARNING logs.
Keep Spawn unless each handler is audited for unscheduled execution.
Do not claim this fixes forged payloads inside legitimate handlers or direct publicVariable channels.
validation: Every registered _serverCommandPV and _clientCommandPV entry resolves to CODE after Init_PublicVariables.sqf.
Smoke one server-bound PVF and one client-bound PVF.
Forged unregistered handler names no-op and log.
git diff --check and wiki validation pass after docs updates.

summary: Make RequestSpecial ICBM/nuke handling server-authoritative before any public-server hardening claim.
implementationShape: Re-derive requester side, commander permission, module enablement, upgrade state, cost and target/base validity server-side.
Debit funds server-side or reject before spawning damage/effects.
Log accepted and rejected requests compactly.
Keep the client Tactical menu as affordance only.
validation: Valid commander launch still works.
Forged non-commander, wrong-side, invalid-target and missing-upgrade requests are rejected.
Accepted/rejected requests are observable in server logs.

summary: Harden direct publicVariable channels separately from the registered WFBE_PVF dispatcher; DR-41 proves this class with ATTACK_WAVE_INIT.
implementationShape: Treat each direct channel as a request, not trusted state.
Re-derive side, requester, cost and effect inputs server-side for attack wave and side-supply mutations.
Keep supply mission direct channels under the dedicated supply cleanup playbook.
Design BattlEye publicvariable.txt as defense-in-depth only after server authority work is understood.
validation: Forged ATTACK_WAVE_INIT supply/side cannot create free or negative-price units.
Wrong-side direct requests are rejected or ignored.
Public-variable channel inventory remains updated after any new direct channel changes.

summary: Choose a coherent server-ledger model for construction, defense, upgrades, player buys, side supply and service/equipment spend paths.
implementationShape: Pick server-side ledger ownership before patching isolated exploits.
Server recomputes price, side, ownership, placement, reward and upgrade dependencies.
Leave client menus as affordance and feedback layers.
Separate player buy redesign from smaller PVF handler hardening.
validation: Legitimate build, defense, upgrade, buy, service and HQ recovery flows still work.
Forged wrong-side, wrong-price and impossible-position payloads are rejected.
Hosted and dedicated locality paths are smoke-tested.
LoadoutManager propagation runs after source mission edits.

summary: Clean up supply mission authority, cooldown casing, dead twin code and PR #1 supply-vehicle reward state before merging supply helicopters; command-center scan narrowing is already source/Vanilla patched and smoke pending.
implementationShape: Retire unused supplyMissionActive.sqf compile path if still dead.
Standardize LastSupplyMissionRun casing.
Server recomputes reward/source from trusted truck/town state where possible.
Guard/remove per-vehicle Killed handlers.

Done source-only: command-center detection now uses nearestObjects [(getPos _associatedSupplyTruck), ["Base_WarfareBUAVterminal"], 80] in Chernarus source and Vanilla Takistan, while the separate player-proximity scan remains broad.
validation: Truck mission works repeatedly and after vehicle reuse.
Destroyed supply vehicle reward pays once.
JIP cooldown query returns current state.
Starter disconnect does not orphan completion tracking.
Source-only: command-center scan uses ["Base_WarfareBUAVterminal"] and player proximity remains intentionally broad or separately justified.
Smoke: both sides' command centers in source/Vanilla and PR #1 truck/heli deliveries still complete.

summary: Smoke the patched paratrooper marker callback and decide whether MASH marker relay should be revived with JIP-safe state or removed.
implementationShape: Smoke current source/Vanilla paratrooper marker registration in-game.
For modded missions, decide whether to propagate the handler file or remove the callback registration from generated variants.
For MASH, either remove dead relay code or revive with server-held marker list, unique names and JIP replay.
validation: Paratrooper drop creates side-filtered client markers on patched missions.
Modded mission marker behavior is no longer ambiguous.
MASH deploy either intentionally creates current/JIP-safe markers or no dead marker relay remains.

summary: Confirm production BattlEye filter posture or document that the repo only ships the AFK kick feature rule.
implementationShape: Ask the server owner whether production BEpath has filters outside the repo.
If in-repo filters are desired, design around both registered WFBE_PVF_* names and direct channels such as kickAFK, attack wave, supply mission, day/night and HQ state.
Do not rely on BattlEye as a substitute for server-side authority.
validation: Owner confirms deployment posture or accepts repo hardening gap.
Legitimate mission traffic is still allowed by any proposed publicvariable.txt design.
Docs keep server authority and BattlEye defense-in-depth separate.

summary: Resolve the source version.sqf generation gap and duplicate Init_Server compile/bind rows if a code owner wants small maintenance cleanup.
implementationShape: Decide whether version.sqf remains generated-only or should have a safe source placeholder.
De-duplicate live Init_Server bind rows only after confirming the second bind is not intentional override behavior.
Keep generated mission folders secondary to Chernarus source and LoadoutManager propagation.
validation: Fresh checkout docs tell a tester how version.sqf appears before preprocessing/packing.
LoadoutManager generation still writes expected terrain version files.
Duplicate bind cleanup does not change function targets or init order.
